metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1763
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 6 shows that SGT when complimented with CSP-alpha and Hsc-70 acts against the protein misfolding (aggregation state) of SNAP-25. Supporting the formation of a trimeric complex in regulation of SNAP-25's half life.

29/01/2018 Pim
- Although the data in Fig.6 show the direct role of SGT-alpha (small glutamine-rich tetratricopeptide repeat protein alpha) in the chaperoning in a cell free system. The relevance for and occurence in vivo is suggested by the IP data in Fig.5. Here SNAP-25 IP resulted in selective CSPα, Hsc70 and SGT or SNARE proteins pulldown from brain homogenates.
- Effects on synaptic transmission is not shown in these or other data since the CSP KO is lethal. A role in SV exocytosis is proposed by the authors. This may be a reasonable inference, but I decided not to annotate to term 'regulation of synaptic vesicle exocytosis'.. Experimental description: Inferred from isolation of the original cDNA for SGT-alpha in PMID:11580898
